assertions.description: container-type accepts the scroll-state value, defining a scroll-state query container.
The container query supports the (stuck: top) feature for sticky elements pinned at the top.
assertions.test: CSS.supports('selector(@container scroll-state(stuck: top) {})') || CSS.supports('@container scroll-state(stuck: top)')

assertions.description: The (snapped: x) / (snapped: y) features query scroll-snap alignment state.
assertions.test: CSS.supports('selector(@container scroll-state(snapped: y) {})') || CSS.supports('@container scroll-state(snapped: y)')

assertions.description: The (scrollable: top) / (scrollable: bottom) features query remaining scroll distance.
assertions.test: CSS.supports('selector(@container scroll-state(scrollable: top) {})') || CSS.supports('@container scroll-state(scrollable: top)')

assertions.description: container-name is supported so scroll-state containers can be addressed by name.
The container shorthand sets container-name and container-type: scroll-state together.
